description: Expected TypeScript semantic errors from BCL generation - ZERO expected since v1.6.0
notes: As of v1.7.0, NrtContractNormalizer is compiler-grade with three key fixes:
1. CLUSTER CONSTRUCTION: Uses Transitive Common Ancestor (TCA) algorithm.
Types are clustered if they share ANY common ancestor, even if that ancestor
is not in the type set. This handles cases where interface A and B both
implement IFoo, but IFoo itself is not emitting the method.
2. COMPILER-GRADE IDENTITY KEYS: Method keys include:
Property keys include IsIndexer flag and indexer parameter signature.
3. CONTRACT DIRECTIONALITY: Bases and interfaces define contracts.
- If ANY base/interface is NotNull → contract is NotNull
- If ANY base/interface is Nullable → contract is Nullable
- If ALL bases are Oblivious and derived has Nullable → lift to Nullable
Derived types incorrectly declaring NotNull do NOT force base to NotNull.
Zero syntax errors (TS1xxx) is always required.
